title: 10 best herbal capsules for flu

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Purple coneflower contains antiviral compounds like cichoric acid and echinacoside, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Immunomodulation: Echinacea purpurea has been found to stimulate the production of white blood cells, such as neutrophils and macrophages, which play a key role in fighting off infections like the flu, while also reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbs' flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce congestion, fever, and body aches associated with the flu, as well as alleviating symptoms of sinusitis and bronchitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with flu because of their unique composition, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's active ingredients, such as echinacea, work to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms like fever, cough, and body aches. Additionally, purple coneflower capsules contain antiviral properties that help combat viral replication, thereby shortening the recovery time. By taking these capsules, individuals can alleviate their flu symptoms and promote faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside and cichoric acid, have been shown to possess immunomodulatory properties, which help boost the immune system to fight off flu viruses.
Alkylphenols: Alkylphenols, such as caffeic acid, have been found to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are widely used because they are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to fight flu symptoms.
Flowers: These are often used due to their high content of alkylamides, which have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, supporting the body's natural defense against flu viruses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the powdered echinacea purpurea material.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters capsules helps with flu because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work together to stimulate the body's immune system and alleviate symptoms. The herbal formula includes ingredients like Andrographis, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate respiratory congestion, while other herbs like Ginger and Echinacea help to boost the body's defenses against viral infections. This powerful combination helps to shorten the duration of flu symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: The capsule's antiviral compounds, such as berberine and glycyrrhizin, help to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause the flu, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the illness.

Immune system stimulation: The herbs in the capsule, like echinacea and goldenseal, stimulate the immune system to produce white blood cells and antibodies, which helps the body fight off the infection more effectively.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in the capsule, such as willow bark and ginger, help to reduce the fever and body aches associated with the flu, making it easier for the body to recover.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: Acts as an antiviral agent by inhibiting the replication of influenza viruses and reducing the severity of flu symptoms.
Dihydroandrographolide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce fever, alleviate body aches, and alleviate respiratory congestion associated with flu.
Ligustilide: Demonstrates antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which help combat the flu virus and alleviate symptoms such as cough, sore throat, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used parts of Andrographis paniculata due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a key bioactive compound responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part of the plant, often used in combination with the leaves, to create a more potent andrographis extract.
Stems: While not as commonly used as leaves or roots, stems may also be used to make andrographis capsules, particularly in traditional or folk medicine practices.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and stems from well-maintained plants.
Clean the harvested material and dry it at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried andrographis paniculata into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of gelatin and 500 milliliters of water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng capsules helps with flu because they contain eleutherosides, which stimulate the immune system to fight off infections. By boosting the body's natural defense mechanisms, Siberian ginseng capsules can help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue. Additionally, these capsules may also enhance energy levels and improve mental clarity, helping individuals to recover from the debilitating effects of the flu virus.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Siberian Ginseng capsules help the body adapt to stress, including physical and immune system stress caused by the flu, allowing it to respond more effectively to infection.
Antiviral activity: The herb has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help prevent the replication of the flu virus in the body, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Immune system enhancement: Siberian Ginseng capsules have immunomodulatory effects, meaning they can stimulate or regulate the immune system's response to infection, helping the body fight off the flu more effectively and recover faster from illness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with flu by exhibiting immunomodulatory and antiviral properties, which aid in boosting the immune system and fighting off the influenza virus.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids help with flu by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the flu virus, thereby alleviating symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with flu by exhibiting antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the replication of the flu virus and reduce inflammation, leading to a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of eleutherosides, which are believed to have adaptogenic properties that help the body fight off flu viruses.
Stems: They are used because they contain a significant amount of eleutherosides, similar to roots, which can aid in immune system support and flu prevention.
Barks: They are used due to their eleutheroside content, which can help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots from a trusted supplier to ensure purity.
Dry the roots further in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the blend and seal with a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Woad capsules helps with flu because they contain natural ingredients that work synergistically to combat the symptoms of flu. The herbal extracts in woad capsules, such as Echinacea and Andrographis, stimulate the immune system to produce cytokines, which fight off the infection. Additionally, the antiviral properties of these herbs help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, cough, and congestion. As a result, woad capsules can provide effective relief from the discomforts associated with the flu.
remedy_properties: Immunomodulation: Echinacea extracts contain compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells to fight off infections like the flu.
Antioxidant activity: Echinacea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by the flu virus, protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and body aches associated with the flu.
remedy_constituents: Isothiocyanates: These compounds are known for their antiviral properties, which help to inhibit the replication of flu viruses and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly isorhapontigenin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which aid in reducing the inflammatory response and oxidative stress associated with flu.
Quercetin: A flavonoid glycoside found in Isatis tinctoria, quercetin has potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms and prevent the progression of flu.
remedy_parts: Isatis tinctoria Leaves: They contain isothiocyanates, which are believed to have antiviral and immunomodulatory properties to help fight flu symptoms.
Isatis tinctoria Roots: They contain isothiocyanates and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate flu symptoms.
Isatis tinctoria Seeds: They contain isothiocyanates and other bioactive compounds that may help boost the immune system and prevent flu infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of isatis tinctoria roots and leaves in a digital scale to ensure accurate dosage.
Dry the isatis tinctoria mixture in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind the dried isatis tinctoria mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 2% magnesium stearate with 10 grams of the ground isatis tinctoria powder to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250 milligrams of the isatis tinctoria powder mixture using a powder filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with flu because they contain bioactive compounds like taraxasterol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, dandelion can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of flu such as fever, headache, and fatigue. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties can help combat viral infections, supporting the body's natural defense against flu viruses and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate fever, headaches, and body aches associated with the flu.

Antiviral properties: Dandelion has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including influenza, which can help shorten the duration of the illness and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Immune-boosting properties: Dandelion is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and calcium, which can help support immune function and increase resistance to infection.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
Phenolic acids (e.g., chlorogenic acid, caffeic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with flu.

Flavonoids (e.g., luteolin, apigenin): These plant-derived compounds exhibit antiviral and immune-boosting properties, which can help combat viral infections and support the body's natural defense mechanisms against flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They contain taraxasterol and other saponins that help to reduce fever and relieve flu symptoms.
Seeds: They contain sesquiterpene lactones which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the collected taraxacum officinale material in a single layer at 25-30°c for 24 to 48 hours.
Grind 100g of the dried taraxacum officinale material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powder with 500mg of gelatin powder and add 10ml of distilled water to create a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of the paste and seal them using a capsule machine in 30 seconds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with flu because they contain active compounds like bioflavonoids, saponins, and triterpenes that have antiviral properties. These compounds help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue. Elder capsules also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. Additionally, their antimicrobial properties may help to prevent secondary infections that can prolong flu recovery time. By taking elder capsules regularly during flu season, you can help your body fight off the infection more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder capsules contain antiviral compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms by inhibiting the replication of viruses.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The bioactive compounds in elder capsules, such as flavonoids and saponins, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like fever, headache, and body aches associated with flu.

Immune system support: Elder capsules are rich in antioxidants and other phytochemicals that can help to boost the immune system's natural defense against infection, reducing the risk of complications and promoting recovery from the flu.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with flu, such as fever and body aches.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antiviral properties, which help inhibit the spread of the flu virus in the body, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
Vitexin: This flavonoid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of the flu, such as congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of antiviral compounds, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help combat flu.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiviral properties, including the presence of sambunigrin, which helps reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
Fruits (or Berries): The fruits are used for their high content of anthocyanins and other antioxidants, which help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation associated with flu.
remedy_recipe: Dry the sambucus nigra flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Crush the dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the powdered sambucus nigra with a binding agent like gelatin and a small amount of water to create a uniform mixture.
Use a capsule filler or a small spoon to fill gelatin capsules with the sambucus nigra mixture.
Store the sambucus nigra capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod capsules helps with flu because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in goldenrod, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the body, alleviating symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and sinus pressure. Additionally, goldenrod's antiviral properties help to combat the underlying flu virus, supporting the immune system and promoting a faster recovery. By taking goldenrod capsules during the onset of flu-like symptoms, individuals can potentially shorten the duration and severity of their illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Goldenseal's berberine compound has been shown to inhibit the growth and replication of various viruses, including those responsible for the common cold and flu, helping to reduce symptoms and shorten duration.
Immune system support: Goldenseal's antimicrobial properties can help stimulate the immune system to produce white blood cells, which fight off infections and inflammation associated with the flu.

Anti-inflammatory action: Goldenseal contains anti-inflammatory compounds like berberine and hydrastis, which may help alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches associated with the flu by reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms by protecting the body from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the viral and bacterial components of flu infections by reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting a faster recovery.

Saponins: These compounds have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate flu symptoms.
Roots: Contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help support the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of solidago virgaurea flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them in a well-ventilated area.
Grind 100 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered solidago virgaurea with 200 grams of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal blend using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 24 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Amla capsules helps with flu because they contain the extract of the Indian gooseberry, which is rich in vitamin C. Vitamin C plays a crucial role in boosting the immune system, thus helping to fight off the influenza virus. The antioxidants present in amla also help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and sore throat. Additionally, amla's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can occur secondary to the flu, making it an effective natural remedy for combating the flu.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Amla's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the flu virus, protecting the body from further damage and promoting faster recovery.

Immune-modulating property: Amla's ability to modulate the immune system helps to boost the body's natural defense against the flu virus, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory property: Amla's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat, nose, and chest, providing relief from common flu symptoms like a sore throat, runny nose, and coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C): Acts as an antioxidant to neutralize free radicals that contribute to flu symptoms, while also supporting the immune system to fight off the infection.

Phenolic Acids (Ellagic Acid, Gallic Acid): Exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to combat the virus and reduce fever, as well as soothe respiratory issues like coughing and sore throat.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties to reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as body aches, fatigue, and congestion, while also supporting the immune system to recover from the infection.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Phyllanthus emblica are rich in vitamin C, which acts as an immune booster and helps prevent the flu.
Leaves: The leaves of Phyllanthus emblica contain antioxidants and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight off flu-causing viruses.
Seeds: The seeds of Phyllanthus emblica contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that help boost the immune system and prevent the flu.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried phyllanthus emblica fruits and sift them through a fine mesh sieve.
Combine the sifted phyllanthus emblica with 125 grams of tapioca starch in a large bowl.
Add 5 grams of magnesium stearate and 2 grams of silicon dioxide to the bowl.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 500 empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture.
Label the capsules with the name phyllanthus emblica and the dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with flu because they provide a concentrated dose of antioxidants, antimicrobial agents, and soothing compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. The herbs used in these capsules, such as Echinacea, Ginger, and Slippery Elm, have been traditionally used to combat the common cold and flu viruses, boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: Herbal tea capsules like Ginger, Turmeric, and Echinacea contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the body, while antioxidants combat free radicals that can cause further damage.

Immune System Boosting: Herbal teas like Elderberry, Echinacea, and Goldenseal contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off viral infections like the flu, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Decongestant and Expectorant Properties: Herbs like Peppermint, Thyme, and Eucalyptus have natural decongestant properties that help ease congestion and coughs by breaking down mucus, while also soothing a sore throat.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have potent antiviral properties that help to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a class of phenolic compounds, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, body aches, and fatigue associated with the flu.

L-theanine: L-theanine, an amino acid found in green tea, has a calming effect on the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany flu symptoms, while also promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves are used to make tea, which is believed to have medicinal properties that can help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
Roots: Camellia sinensis roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues, which may be related to flu.
Seeds: Camellia sinensis seeds are sometimes used in herbal medicine to treat fever and other health issues, possibly due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves and sift them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris.
Combine the sifted leaves with 100 milliliters of water in a heat-proof glass bowl and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 10 minutes then let it steep for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids then measure out 10 milliliters of the liquid extract.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the camellia sinensis extract per capsule and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ivy capsules helps with flu because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. The extracts from the ivy plant have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying infection and reduce the severity of symptoms. Additionally, herbal ivy capsules may also help to soothe a sore throat and calm a cough, providing relief from the discomfort associated with the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal ivy capsules contain saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses, helping to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in herbal ivy capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate congestion, coughing, and body aches commonly associated with the flu.

Mucolytic properties: Herbal ivy capsules contain mucilages that can help to break down mucus and relieve respiratory congestion, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm, allowing for faster recovery from the flu.
remedy_constituents: Aucubin: Aucubin is a glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Hedera helix alkaloids: The alkaloids present in Hedera helix capsules, such as isopiperitenone, may have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's immune response and fight off the flu virus.

Volatile terpenes (e.g., borneol, borneol acetate): These terpenes have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help combat the flu virus and reduce associated symptoms such as fever and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antiviral properties, particularly against the influenza virus.
Buds: They are used for their potential to boost the immune system, which helps in fighting flu.
Stems: They are used due to their antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing inflammation and relieving flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried hedera helix leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powder with 250ml of vegetable glycerin and 50g of gelatin in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes over medium heat until the gelatin dissolves and thickens.
Allow the mixture to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before filling capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with flu because they possess antiviral properties that combat the virus, reducing its severity and duration. The capsules also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe a sore throat and relieve congestion. Additionally, lemon verbena has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, which can help prevent future bouts of illness. By taking herbal lemon verbena capsules regularly during flu season, individuals can proactively support their overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon verbena's antiviral compounds, such as citral and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause the flu, by interfering with their ability to infect cells.

Inflammation reduction: Lemon verbena contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms such as fever, headache, and sore throat associated with the flu.
Immune system support: The herb's immunomodulatory effects can help stimulate the immune system to fight off infection, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms, and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with flu.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and vitamins, they provide immunity-boosting effects and may help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present and gently dry them using paper towels or clean cloth for two hours.
Blend the dried herbs with 50 grams of organic cornstarch powder in a glass grinder for five minutes until it forms a consistent mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one teaspoon of the herb mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency for up to six months.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to three years.
Take two capsules thrice daily for five days as needed or until flu symptoms subside and then consult with a healthcare professional for further guidance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed capsules helps with flu because its active compounds, such as beraprost and flavonoids, possess antiviral properties that can effectively combat the influenza virus. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the replication of viral RNA, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms like fever, cough, and sore throat. Additionally, joe pye weed's anti-inflammatory effects can help alleviate body aches and congestion, making it an excellent natural remedy for fighting off the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains antiviral compounds like triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that can help combat viral infections such as the flu by inhibiting virus replication and reducing inflammation.

Expectorant properties: The herb has natural expectorant properties, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm, a common symptom of the flu.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Joe Pye Weed's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce the body's inflammatory response to infection, alleviating symptoms like fever, chills, and body aches associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce fever and alleviate respiratory congestion associated with the flu.
Seeds: Containing triterpenoid saponins, seeds have been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the flu virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry eupatorium perfoliatum flowers in a warm place for two weeks or until they become brittle.
Grind dried eupatorium perfoliatum flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 3000mg of the powdered eupatorium perfoliatum with 1000mg of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the eupatorium perfoliatum-silica mixture and seal tightly.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with flu because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and coughs. The herbal extract also has expectorant properties, helping to loosen mucus and reduce congestion in the chest and nasal passages. Additionally, licorice root has antiviral properties that may help combat the underlying infection, reducing the severity and duration of flu-like symptoms. By taking herbal licorice capsules regularly, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and distress caused by the flu.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain flavonoids and saponins that reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and congestion.

Expectorant properties: The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve respiratory congestion associated with flu.
Antiviral properties: Licorice root has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause the flu, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing the severity of flu symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, of licorice have been used for centuries to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and congestion, common symptoms of the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reputable source.
Combine the powdered root with 100 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Mix 200 milligrams of the cooled liquid extract with 500 milligrams of vegetable cellulose capsule material and fill empty gelatin capsules.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with flu because they contain antimicrobial properties that combat viral infections. The bioactive compounds in onions, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms like fever, cough, and congestion. Additionally, onion extract has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate body aches and fatigue associated with flu. By supplementing with herbal onion capsules, individuals can support their immune system and reduce the risk of complications from flu infection.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Onion contains compounds like quercetin, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause the flu.
Mucus-clearing and expectorant properties: The sulfur compounds in onions can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Onion's polyphenol compounds, such as kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like fever, headache, and body aches associated with flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Onion roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and colds, making them a prime candidate for creating medicinal capsules for flu.
Barks: The barks of the onion plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and congestion associated with the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh onion bulbs allium cepa weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the onion bulbs in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Grind dried onion bulbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix the powdered onion with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create an infusion and steep for 10 minutes.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the cooled infusion mixture at a dosage of 250 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Snowdrops capsules helps with flu because they contain extracts of Galanthus nivalis, a natural antiviral agent that has been used for centuries to combat respiratory infections. The active compounds in snowdrops, such as galantamine and lycorine, have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms like fever, cough, and congestion. Additionally, the capsules may also help to boost the body's natural defenses against viral infections, reducing the severity and duration of flu episodes.
remedy_properties: Antiviral Properties: Herbal Snowdrops capsules contain Galanthamine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antiviral properties, which helps to combat the influenza virus and reduce its replication, thereby alleviating symptoms of the flu.

Expectorant Properties: The capsules also contain compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are known for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with the flu.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal Snowdrops capsules contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation in the body, thereby alleviating symptoms like fever, headaches, and body aches commonly associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the snowdrops plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and relieving body aches associated with flu.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the snowdrops plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including congestion and coughs, which are common symptoms of the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest galanthus nivalis flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 3-4 hours or until crispy.
Grind the dried galanthus nivalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered galanthus nivalis with 500mg of gelatin capsules, filling each capsule to 80% capacity.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container, protecting from light and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with flu because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce fever, alleviate body aches, and soothe congestion. The plantain extract also has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the virus, preventing its spread throughout the body. Additionally, herbal plantain capsules can boost the immune system, enabling it to fight off the infection more effectively. By taking these capsules, individuals can reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, allowing them to recover faster and feel better sooner.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which helps to relieve body aches and fever associated with the flu.

Antiviral properties: The plant's extracts have been shown to inhibit the replication of viral pathogens, including those responsible for the flu, thereby helping to shorten the duration and severity of the illness.

Immune-boosting properties: Plantain is rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help support immune function, which can aid in fighting off infections and reducing the risk of complications from the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating congestion associated with flu.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the plantain plant is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the flu virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried plantago major leaves and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered plantago major with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 10-15 minutes over low heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 5-7 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the strained liquid extract and seal them tightly within 2 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or until needed and consume as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose capsules helps with flu because of its antiviral properties, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses in the body. The herbal extract contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating their natural defenses against infection. Additionally, beach rose has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as sore throat, cough, and congestion. As a result, taking beach rose capsules can help alleviate the severity and duration of flu-like symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Beach Rose capsules have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat the influenza virus and reduce its replication in the body.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The capsules may help alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches associated with the flu by reducing inflammation and soothing the respiratory tract.

Immune system support: Beach Rose is believed to have immunomodulatory properties, which can help boost the immune system's natural defenses against infections like the flu, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with the flu.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion, which are common symptoms of the flu.
remedy_recipe: Collect rosa rugosa flowers at peak bloom and air-dry them in a warm, dry place for several weeks.
Grind dried rosa rugosa flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 parts ground rosa rugosa with 90 parts pure gelatin in a small bowl, allowing mixture to sit for at least an hour.
Add 200ml of boiling water to the gelatin mixture, stirring until gelatin is fully dissolved, then let cool and set for several hours.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 500mg of the rosa rugosa-gelatin mixture, sealing caps tightly and storing in a cool, dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for flu are natural supplements that aid in reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, cough, and fatigue. They work by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. The best herbal capsules for flu include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea angustifolia, Andrographis paniculata, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea pallida, Astragalus membranaceus, Sambucus nigra, Ginkgo biloba, and Panax ginseng. If left untreated, flu can lead to missed work, school, and social events, causing emotional distress, financial losses, and a weakened immune system, ultimately affecting overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to flu that are treated with herbal capsules are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, sinusitis, and pneumonia. Herbal supplements like andrographis, echinacea, and propolis have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms like coughing, congestion, and fever. Other conditions that can be managed with herbal capsules include upper respiratory tract infections, common colds, and allergies. These natural remedies often offer a safe and effective alternative to conventional medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with flu is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Elderberry. Echinacea enhances the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Ginger aids in digestion and reduces nausea, while also providing anti-inflammatory properties. Elderberry contains antiviral compounds that help combat the flu virus, making it an effective natural remedy. This combination can help alleviate symptoms, boost immunity, and promote a faster recovery from the flu.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Medicinal Research Reviews, herbal capsules have shown significant therapeutic and preventive effects against influenza due to their ability to target both the virus and the host. The study, which reviewed chemical ingredients, crude extracts, and traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) prescriptions with anti-influenza activity from 2010 to 2019, found that Lianhuaqingwen capsule exerts its anti-influenza activity by regulating the immune response to interfere with both viral and host reactions. This suggests that herbal capsules, such as Lianhuaqingwen, could be a promising alternative candidate for treating influenza virus infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with flu because they contain compounds that boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. The herb's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help fight off the flu virus. By stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, Echinacea purpurea capsules support the body's natural defenses against infection, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. This natural remedy can provide relief from flu-related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: They help stimulate the body's immune response, which is essential for fighting off flu viruses.
Triterpenoid glycosides (e.g., echinacoside): They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Iridoid glycosides (e.g., echinulin): They have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to the flu virus, thereby reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for flu because they contain high levels of echinacoside and alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and boost the immune system.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make capsules for flu because they are rich in echinacein, a compound thought to have antiviral properties and help fight off infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for flu because they contain flavonoids and other polyphenols, which may help reduce inflammation and support immune function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Dry 100g of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 400mg of the powder with 400mg of a gelatin capsule filler and 100mg of a cellulose capsule coating in a mortar and pestle.
Fill 100 capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine with a speed of 100 capsules per minute.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with flu because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, gingerol, present in these capsules has been shown to reduce congestion, ease nausea, and alleviate body aches associated with flu. Additionally, ginger's ability to boost the immune system and fight off viral infections makes it an effective remedy for flu symptoms. Its natural and gentle approach also makes it an appealing alternative to conventional medications for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help alleviate flu symptoms by reducing fever and relieving respiratory tract congestion.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which aid in reducing the severity of flu symptoms, such as cough and sore throat.
Zingiberene: As a sesquiterpene, zingiberene exhibits antiviral activity, which helps combat the flu virus by inhibiting its replication and reducing the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate flu symptoms.
Buds: They are used as they contain a higher concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds than mature rhizomes, making them effective in combating flu.
Roots: They are used as they contain a mixture of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale rhizomes in the fall season when the foliage starts to turn yellow and die back naturally.
Clean and dry the harvested rhizomes in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed grinding machine for 10 minutes at 5000 rpm.
Mix the powdered rhizome with a filler material like rice flour in a 1:1 ratio to achieve a total weight of 500mg per capsule.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the zingiber officinale powder mixture and seal the capsules immediately to prevent moisture from entering.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia capsules helps with flu because they contain a high concentration of alkylamides, which stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off invading pathogens. The roots of the Echinacea plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate congestion associated with flu. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia has been traditionally used to shorten the duration and severity of flu symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for maintaining overall health and well-being during cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms by increasing the production of white blood cells.
Alkylamides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and congestion associated with the flu.

Polysaccharides: These compounds can stimulate the immune system and increase the production of cytokines, which are proteins that help to fight off infections and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of echinacoside and alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.
Seeds: They contain alkaloids and glycosides, which may contribute to the plant's potential immune-boosting effects.
Roots: They also contain a high concentration of phenolic acids, such as chicoric acid, which may have antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots at the end of the blooming season when the plants are in full flower.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground root powder with a binding agent like gelatin or cellulose in a 1:1 ratio by weight for capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixed powder using a capsule filling machine or a small spoon and store in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata capsules helps with flu because they have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to boost the immune system and alleviate flu symptoms. The herb contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce fever, soothe a sore throat, and ease congestion. Studies have shown that Andrographis paniculata can also reduce the duration and severity of flu symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for flu prevention and treatment. Its effects can aid in a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Neoheleriolin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Andrographolide: It has antiviral properties that inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, thereby reducing the severity of flu symptoms.
14-deoxyandrographolide: It has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier to fight off the flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a key compound responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Stems: The stems contain andrographolide as well, making them a secondary choice for extracting the active compound.
Roots: Although less commonly used, roots of Andrographis paniculata are also used to make capsules for their andrographolide content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried andrographis paniculata root and leaf material from a reputable source.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 parts of the powder with 1 part of gelatin or vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and dispense into empty capsules using the machine for 10 minutes.
Store the andrographis paniculata capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with flu because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compounds in Licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughs associated with flu. Additionally, its antiviral properties help to combat the underlying viral infection, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules a popular natural remedy for alleviating flu symptoms and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help with flu by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can alleviate symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Liquiritin and its derivatives: These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help combat the viral infection and alleviate symptoms such as sore throat and cough.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever and body aches, by modulating the body's cytokine response.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate flu symptoms.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin in roots also has a soothing effect on the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe and recover from flu.
Roots: The root's expectorant properties help in thinning mucus and relieving congestion, a common symptom of the flu.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier to ensure quality.
Grind 250 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered roots with 500 grams of gelatin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place to maintain their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida capsules helps with flu because of its potent immunomodulatory properties. It stimulates the production of white blood cells, which are essential for fighting off viral infections. Echinacea pallida's alkylamides and polysaccharides work synergistically to enhance the body's natural defenses, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate congestion, coughs, and body aches associated with the flu, making it a popular natural remedy for seasonal flu prevention and treatment.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which help to boost the body's immune response to fight off flu viruses.
Alkylamides: These constituents have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Caffeic Acid: This phenolic acid has been reported to have antiviral properties, which can help to inhibit the replication of flu viruses and reduce the duration of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea pallida are commonly used due to their high concentration of alkylamides and phenolic compounds, which are believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or underground stems) of Echinacea pallida are also widely used as they contain a high amount of polysaccharides and other bioactive compounds that support immune function.

Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea pallida are used for their mild anti-inflammatory properties and their ability to stimulate the immune system, although they are less commonly used than roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried echinacea pallida roots and flowers in the fall season.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder within 5 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powdered echinacea with 4 grams of vegetable cellulose in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 to 1 gram of the echinacea mixture within 10 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with flu because they stimulate the immune system, increasing the body's natural defenses against infections. The herb contains saponins, flavonoids, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms. By boosting the immune system and fighting off free radicals, Astragalus membranaceus capsules may help alleviate flu-related congestion, cough, and fatigue, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. Its adaptogenic properties also help manage stress.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever and body aches.
Isoliquiritigenin (ILG): ILG has immune-boosting properties, which can help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms against the flu virus.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to the flu virus and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which contribute to the herb's immunity-boosting properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus contain high levels of astragalosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, making them useful in flu prevention.
Seeds: Astragalus membranaceus seeds are rich in astragalosides and other bioactive compounds that help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh astragalus membranaceus roots in the spring or fall season when the plant is dormant.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind 4-6 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root powder with 100-200 mg of silica gel to fill capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder-silica gel mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with flu because they contain high levels of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to boost the immune system, reduce the severity of flu symptoms, and prevent the replication of the influenza virus. By taking Sambucus nigra capsules, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and body aches, and speed up their recovery from the flu.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids help reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, which can aid in fighting off flu viruses and reducing its severity.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids possess antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of flu viruses and other pathogens in the body.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of the flu and support the body's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.

Fruits (Elderberries): The fruits (elderberries) are used due to their high content of anthocyanins, which have been shown to have antiviral properties and can help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of tapioca starch and 200 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Add 20 grams of silicon dioxide as a filler to the mixture and stir well with a spoon for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill 500 capsules with the powder mixture at a speed of 100 capsules per minute.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years after production.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with flu because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have potent antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the flu virus. These compounds also improve blood circulation and boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the infection more effectively. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as congestion and coughing, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms and promote overall recovery.
Terpenoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help combat the underlying viral infection and reduce the risk of complications.

Bilobalide: This triterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the flu virus, thereby reducing the risk of long-term damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have medicinal properties.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high concentration of ginkgolides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate flu symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are also used due to their ability to improve blood circulation, which can aid in the recovery from flu by promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_recipe: Crush 250 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure even particle size and remove lumps completely.
Mix 10 grams of the sifted ginkgo biloba powder with 20 grams of vegetable cellulose using a spoon.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with flu because they contain active compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off viral infections. The capsules also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, cough, and body aches. Additionally, Panax ginseng has been shown to have antiviral properties that can directly inhibit the replication of flu viruses, making it a potential natural remedy for preventing and treating the flu.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms and prevent the spread of the virus.
Panaxans: Panaxans have been shown to possess immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the immune system and fight off flu-causing pathogens.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Panax ginseng have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue associated with flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have medicinal properties.
Barks: The barks are also used, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, which can help alleviate flu symptoms.
Stems: The stems are occasionally used, but to a lesser extent than the roots and barks, as they may contain lower concentrations of ginsenosides.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2000mg of panax ginseng root into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground panax ginseng powder with 100mg of starch and 10mg of silica gel in a bowl.
Add a small amount of water to the mixture and stir until it forms a thick paste.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the panax ginseng mixture.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place and consume one capsule twice daily with water.
